submitted_payload.description: Free and open-source composable loop framework and AI orchestrator for autonomous coding. Strong default workflow for planning, implementation, and verification, with Codeberg as the primary repo and GitHub as the mirror.
submitted_payload.notes: Primary listing target should be the Codeberg repo; GitHub is the mirror repo only.

verification.execution_method: Browser-run Bubble form submission via Playwright against the live submit page.
verification.confirmation_state: success screen rendered with Back To Site CTA after submit

why_this_action: The May 23, 2026 audit still shows Codeberg adoption flat, so a fresh executable directory lane is higher leverage than more internal-only drafting.
ToolScout is a developer-facing AI tools directory with an unauthenticated live submit flow that was not yet present in outreach-log.md.
The submission preserves Codeberg as the primary destination and keeps GitHub as mirror-only context.
expected_outcome: Qualified developers discover Ralph Workflow through a fresh AI/developer directory that links to the primary Codeberg repo.
replacement_condition: If there is no live ToolScout listing or referral evidence by the backlink review date, treat this lane as pending-only and spend the next fresh-action slot on another untouched executable distribution lane.

changes: added ToolScout as a tracked easy-submit lane at the correct live path https://toolscout.ai/submit
registered toolscout.ai as a validated autonomous submit host for future distribution passes
updated channel_discovery.json so future lane selection no longer misses ToolScout
verification: verified live browser-run submission success state on https://toolscout.ai/submit
